intent: process the first sample with scanpy

blocks.text: I'll process the first sample (Patient 145, Day 0) with scanpy using the standard QC → filtering → normalization → HVG → PCA → clustering → UMAP workflow.

blocks.content: {"status": "ok", "name": "scrna-qc-clustering", "body": "scRNA-seq QC+clustering (scanpy): read 10x; calculate_qc_metrics; filter n_genes>=200 & pct_mito<0.20; normalize_total(1e4)+log1p; highly_variable_genes; pca; neighbors; umap; leiden; rank_genes_groups. Plots: HVG plot and PCA variance side-by-side; UMAP colored by leiden; use sc.pl.* not hand-rolled matplotlib."}

blocks.text: Perfect! Now let me present the plan:

input.title: scanpy QC+clustering for Patient 145 Day 0
input.summary: Standard scanpy processing of the first COVID-19 sample: QC metrics, filtering, normalization, HVG selection, PCA, Leiden clustering, UMAP visualization, and cluster marker genes.
input.rationale: End-to-end single-sample scRNA-seq processing to generate quality-controlled, normalized counts and initial cluster assignment for downstream analysis.
input.assumptions:
- Using first sample only (GSM5746259, Patient 145 Day 0, 7,532 cells)
- Standard filtering: n_genes ≥ 200, mitochondrial fraction < 20%
- Normalization to 10⁴ counts per cell + log transform
- Leiden clustering (default parameters)
- Human PBMC data (mitochondrial genes expected, no special annotation needed for QC)

steps.title: Load the first sample
steps.description: Extract Patient 145 Day 0 from the combined dataset

steps.title: Calculate QC metrics
steps.description: Add n_genes, n_counts, percent mitochondrial, percent ribosomal to obs

steps.title: Filter cells
expected_outputs: Filtered object; cell count before/after reported

steps.title: Normalize and log-transform
expected_outputs: Raw counts moved to .layers, normalized in .X

steps.title: Scale, PCA, neighbors, UMAP
steps.description: Scale, PCA (50 components), k-NN graph, UMAP reduction

steps.title: Rank cluster markers
steps.description: Wilcoxon test for differentially expressed genes per cluster
